senses.definition: plural form of sambos, a term sometimes used for a snack pastry similar to samosas in some dialects, or a misspelling of sambusas; in other contexts it may be a regional plural for sambosa/sambosa-like pastries.
examples: We bought a plate of sambos from the street stall.
In the cookbook, sambos are described as triangular fried pastries.

senses.definition: an alternative spelling of sambuk, referring to a type of small dhow (traditional sailing vessel) used in the Arabian region.
examples: The fishermen sailed in a sambouk along the coast.
Old maps show a sambouk moored near the harbor.

senses.definition: variant spelling of sambousek, a pastry filled with meat or vegetables common in Middle Eastern cuisines.
examples: We shared several sambouse as appetizers.
The platter included spinach sambouse with yogurt dipping sauce.

senses.definition: astrong Italian anise-flavored liqueur usually served neat or with coffee beans.
examples: We finished the meal with a shot of sambuca.
The bartender poured sambuca and lit the three coffee beans on top.

senses.definition: the botanical family that includes elder trees and shrubs, known for species like Sambucus.
examples: The garden features plants from the Sambucaceae family.
Researchers study the berries of Sambucus within Sambucaceae.

senses.definition: plural of sambuca (the liqueur) or plural of Sambucus-related items depending on context; commonly refers to the liqueur in casual usage.
examples: We ordered two sambucas to share after dinner.
The menu lists sambucas as a digestive option.

senses.definition: a genus of plants in the elder family, including elderberries.
examples: Sambucus leaves can be used in herbal remedies.
Elderflower is derived from Sambucus blossoms.

senses.definition: alternative form of sambuk, a type of dhow—a traditional sailing vessel in the Arabian area.
examples: The fleet included several sambuk ships.
Sambuk design features a curved keel.

senses.definition: a historical or dialect form referring to the act of mounting or lifting; in some dialects, related to a boat or a weapon term; extremely rare in modern usage.
examples: Historical texts mention the term sambuke in nautical contexts.
In old Arabic manuscripts, sambuke appears as a verb form.

senses.definition: plural of sambuke or sambuk, referring to multiple dhow-type vessels or archaic uses.
examples: Ancient harbors showed fleets of sambukes.
The model ships included several carved sambukes.

senses.definition: a term found in certain languages or proper nouns; in some contexts it refers to a specific plant, instrument, or cultural reference, but it is highly context-dependent and uncommon in standard English.
examples: The ethnobotanical text mentions sambul plant in region X.
A musician named Sambul performed at the festival.

senses.definition: a chemical compound (glucoside) found in certain plants; used in scientific contexts.
examples: Researchers isolated sambunigrin from the plant sample.
Sambunigrin has been studied for its biochemical properties.

senses.definition: a type of drum used in South Asian or Middle Eastern music; a percussion instrument with a deep, resonant sound.
examples: The band featured a sambur in the traditional ensemble.
The drummer struck the sambur to keep the rhythm.

senses.definition: plural of sambur; multiple samburs in a musical context.
examples: The ensemble included two samburs and a dhol.
Old recordings feature several samburs in the rhythm section.

senses.definition: a member of the Samburu people, a pastoralist ethnic group in Kenya; also used as a proper noun for the language or culture.
examples: A Samburu guide led us through the region.
The Samburu dress colorful beaded jewelry.

senses.definition: not different or distinct; identical or alike in amount, size, or appearance.
examples: These two shirts are the same color.
We arrived at the same time.

senses.definition: a person or thing that is alike or has shared qualities; also a term used to refer to a person’s equal in some context.
examples: Each student stood beside his same.
The two teams were of the same rank.

senses.definition: to the same extent or degree; likewise.
examples: I like apples; I enjoy bananas too, and so do you, same.
She was tired, and he was tired as well, same.

senses.definition: one of the Hebrew alphabet letters, the ninth letter; used in religious or linguistic contexts.
examples: Samech is the Hebrew letter representing a circular sound.
In Hebrew, samech is the 15th letter of the alphabet in some numeric orders.

senses.definition: plural of samech; multiple occurrences of the Hebrew letter in text or discussion.
examples: The manuscript uses several samechs in a row.
He inscribed several samechs for emphasis.

senses.definition: an alternative transliteration of the Hebrew letter samekh; used in linguistic or religious contexts.
examples: The letter samek appears in the word for 'support' in Hebrew.
Transliteration sometimes uses samek instead of samekh.

senses.definition: the Hebrew letter samekh, 15th in some orders, used in religious and linguistic contexts.
examples: The word ends with a samekh.
In Hebrew script, samekh has a circular shape.

senses.definition: plural of samekh; multiple occurrences of the Hebrew letter.
examples: The scroll contains several samekhs.
Calligraphers practice forming various samekhs.

senses.definition: variant plural form of samek/samekh; refers to Hebrew letter occurrences.
examples: The manuscript shows several sameks.
Students learn how to write sameks correctly.

senses.definition: an ancient or alternative spelling of sammel, or a place/name in certain languages; context-dependent and uncommon.
examples: The document mentions a place named Samel.
Samel appears in historical records.

senses.definition: the quality of being samel or similar; a rarely used term in modern English.
examples: The essay discusses the sameliness of the two theories.
Sameliness is not widely recognized in standard dictionaries.

senses.definition: in the same way; similarly.
examples: She did her homework and samely helped her brother.
If you understand that, samely apply it to another problem.

senses.definition: in the same way; similarly (alt form).
examples: The two methods yield samely results.
Proceed samely with the second step.
